given: a warehouse operator viewing the home screen stock table with at least one row
then: each quantity is right-aligned within its column so the digits line up for scanning

independence.rationale: AC1 asserts the table lists SKU, location and quantity; this adds the distinct visual outcome that the quantity values are right-aligned, which can fail (left-aligned digits) while the table still renders every row correctly.

architectural_notes: E2E: a pure presentation outcome owned entirely by the React SPA (client/) component that renders the listing table; the quantity column's right-alignment is a client styling concern, never a boundary/service/domain responsibility (app/routes returns JSON, no formatting). No new server path beyond the AC1 read; realizes the design's rule that all view formatting lives in the client layer, keeping business logic out of templates. Verified through the client Vitest/Playwright harness (NFR-F1-spa-json-boundary), the digits aligning over real seeded rows.
